patterns.description: Map skills to development phases via slash commands. Users get instant clarity.
Treat SKILL.md as trainable parameters of a frozen agent. Use rollout-reflect-aggregate-select-update-evaluate loop.
Always provide install instructions for ALL platforms in collapsible details.
Include quantified scope in description. Numbers signal depth.
Offensive security skills consistently get 1K-3K stars. Underserved niche.
Skills that improve OTHER skills are novel and high-value.
Curated collections get 10K-146K stars. Require ongoing curation.
Deep expertise in one domain beats shallow coverage of many.
Single SKILL.md repos do NOT get stars. Collections with proper docs, CONTRIBUTING, validation, platform guides get stars.
Top repos have CONTRIBUTING.md with anatomy, required sections, code examples required.
Need: getting-started.md, skill-anatomy.md, platform-specific setup guides.
Automated validation signals engineering rigor.
Every skill needs: Overview, When to Use, Quick Start, Process, Common Mistakes, Verification.
SKILL.md = essentials. reference.md = depth.
Top repos include AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md for AI-native discoverability.

The 10 must-have skills for 2026: frontend-design (277K installs), browser-use, code-reviewer, Remotion, Google Workspace, Valyu, Antigravity collection, PlanetScale, Shannon pentester, Excalidraw diagrams.
Skills activate via description triggers OR slash commands. Best skills have BOTH: auto-trigger from task + explicit /command.
Skills can't express their UI needs for human input. Proposed fix: human-interactions field in spec. This is an UNSOLVED PROBLEM in the ecosystem.
Code examples must be copy-paste ready. No pseudocode, no placeholders. Real imports, real function signatures, real error handling.
Every skill needs a Verification section: how to PROVE it worked. Checklist format. Without this, agents claim success without evidence.
README must show Before (pain) / After (relief). Makes value instantly clear.
GitHub DNS fails intermittently. Always retry with HTTP/1.1 fallback. If DNS fails 3x, network is down — work locally and push later.

philosophies.text: Skills encode the workflows that senior engineers use — packaged so AI agents follow them consistently
Treat the skill document as the trainable state of a frozen agent
A raw agent without skills is like a senior engineer on day one: brilliant, but missing all the project-specific context
One SKILL.md, every platform — cross-platform is the standard
A single SKILL.md is not a product. An ecosystem is a product.
Evidence before claims, always. No completion claims without fresh verification evidence.
Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass. Applied to skills: test the skill with pressure scenarios before deploying.

techniques.name: Slash command mapping
techniques.description: 5-7 slash commands mapping to workflow phases.

techniques.name: Skill validation script
techniques.description: Automated checks: frontmatter, lines, sections, code, placeholders.

techniques.name: Anti-laziness scanner
techniques.description: For each skill ask: what would make this 10x better? What adjacent skill should exist?

techniques.name: Exa semantic search for gap discovery
techniques.description: Query: 'developers wish existed 2026 pain points' + 'most useful AI skills' to find real unmet needs.
